name: The Night Market
text:
Once a year, when the traders come, the market stays open after dark. Lanterns on strings. Foreign smells — spices you can't name, oils that make the air shimmer, something roasting that's definitely not from any animal you've seen.

You're supposed to be in bed. You're not.

The crowd is a forest of legs and elbows. Someone is singing in a language that makes your teeth vibrate. A woman with ink-dark hands is drawing something on a man's arm — not a picture, a pattern, and it's glowing faintly. She catches you staring and winks.

A Rootwalker boy about your age is sitting on a barrel, eating something wrapped in a leaf. He holds out a second one without looking at you. 'Take it. It's better if you don't ask what it is.'

You take it. He's right.
